rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LEON J. WILLIAMS REVOCABLE TRUST DATED 10/5/99 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To First Amended Petition By Betty Williams
rulings.Rulings: Respondent's request for judicial notice is granted under CCP section 452 subsections (d) and (h). Demurrer to the First Cause of Action is overruled. A fair reading of this cause of action does not yield an allegation that Respondent and decedent entered into an oral agreement for sale or transfer of the real property in 1974. Demurrer to the Second Cause of Action for declaratory relief is overruled. Demurrer to the Third Cause of Action is sustained with leave to amend for Petitioners to plead sufficiently the elements of hostility to the co-ownership title, open claim by decedent to color of title or claim of right to the property, and ouster of the recorded co-owner (see Hacienda Ranch Homes, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (2011) 198 Cal. App. 4th 1122, 1128-29, and Dimmick v. Dimmick (1962) 58 Cal. 2d 417, 422). Demurrer to the Fourth Cause of Action is sustained as to Respondent Ms. Williams without leave to amend, as she is not the trustee of the Williams Trust, and Petitioners have represented that are not bringing this cause of action against her. The Demurrer to the Fifth Cause of Action is sustained as to Ms. Williams without leave to amend, as she is not the trustee of the Williams Trust, and Petitioners have represented that are not bringing this cause of action against her. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3772) no later than 4:00 p.m. on August 12, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate.
